column2: Q1: Welcome to Resume Genius's Job Seekers Survey! We are conducting this research to gain a deeper understanding of how U.S. job seekers are navigating today’s market. This survey should take less than 5 minutes to complete. Your responses will be kept confidential and used only for research purposes. We appreciate your time and valuable input. To ensure the authenticity and accuracy of this survey’s results, please answer based on your actual experiences and genuine opinions. By submitting the questionnaire, you confirm that all responses are your own truthful views and agree that the information provided may be used for analysis and research purposes.
column3: Q2: What industry best describes the job you're primarily applying in? (Select the company’s industry, not your job function.)
column4: Q3: As of today, how long have you been actively searching for a job?
column5: Q4: Around how many job applications have you submitted during your current job search?
column6: Q5: On average, how much time do you spend tailoring your resume for a single job application?
column7: Q6: What step of the job search do you find most stressful?
column8: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Attending online or in-person professional networking events (Facebook Events, Meetup, etc.)
column9: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Going on online job boards (Indeed, LinkedIn Jobs, etc.)
column10: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Applying on company career pages
column11: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Talking with recruiters or staffing agencies
column12: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Going through referrals
column13: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Cold emailing or direct outreach
column14: Q7: Which job search methods are you currently using?_Finding job opportunities using social media (Twitter/X, TikTok, etc.)
column15: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Remembering tasks and achievements from past jobs
column16: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Writing strong bullet points
column17: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Tailoring my resume to the job
column18: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Making sure my resume is optimized for applicant tracking systems (ATS)
column19: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Turning my experience into accomplishments
column20: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Adding hard numbers to show results (percentages, dollar amounts, etc.) on my resume
column21: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Formatting and design
column22: Q8: What are the hardest parts of updating your resume?_Keeping it concise
column23: Q9: Please indicate how much you agree with the following statements about resumes and applicant tracking systems (ATS). :: I understand how Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) work:
column24: Q9: Please indicate how much you agree with the following statements about resumes and applicant tracking systems (ATS). :: I feel confident my resume is optimized for ATS:
column25: Q9: Please indicate how much you agree with the following statements about resumes and applicant tracking systems (ATS). :: I worry my resume will be rejected by an ATS before a human sees it:

column27: Q10: How often do you follow common resume advice? :: "Keep your resume to one page.":
column28: Q10: How often do you follow common resume advice? :: "Tailor your resume for each job.":
column29: Q10: How often do you follow common resume advice? :: "Use keywords from the job description.":
column30: Q10: How often do you follow common resume advice? :: "Include a resume summary at the top of your resume.":
column31: Q10: How often do you follow common resume advice? :: "Use accomplishments and hard numbers to show results.":
column32: Q11: Have you used AI tools to help with your job applications?
column33: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Writing my resume
column34: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Writing my cover letter
column35: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Editing my job application
column36: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Preparing for interviews
column37: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Using AI to answer interview questions for me during an interview
column38: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Finishing a skills-based test or assessment on my behalf
column39: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Finding jobs to apply for
column40: Q12: Which parts of the job search have you used AI for?_Receiving salary or negotiation advice
column41: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Not receiving a response after submitting a job application
column42: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Having long delays between interview stages
column43: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Not receiving a response after completing one or more interviews
column44: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Being put through too many interview rounds
column45: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Being asked to complete unpaid assignments or tests
column46: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Being required to complete one-way video or AI-recorded interviews
column47: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Applying to jobs where the salary is not listed
column48: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Receiving automated or AI-generated rejection messages
column49: Q13: Which recruiting practices frustrate you the most?_Discovering the role differs significantly from the job posting during the hiring process
column50: Q14: Have you ever added a skill to your resume that you planned to learn later if you got the job?
column51: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_AI usage (Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, etc.)
column52: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Programming languages (Python, C++, JavaScript, etc.)
column53: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Microsoft Office or Google Workspace

column55: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Design tools (Figma, Canva, etc)
column56: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Collaboration platforms (Jira, Slack, Trello, etc.)
column57: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Data or analytics tools (Excel, SQL, Tableau, etc.)
column58: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Management skills and experience
column59: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Foreign language skills
column60: Q15: Which skills did you list that you planned to learn after being hired?_Soft skills (creativity, problem-solving, time management, etc.)

column68: Q17: How has job searching affected your mental health?

column72: Q18: Which factors have negatively affected your mental health during your job search?_Staying motivated
column73: Q18: Which factors have negatively affected your mental health during your job search?_Feeling unqualified
column74: Q18: Which factors have negatively affected your mental health during your job search?_None of the above

column84: Q22: Have you ever exaggerated or lied during a job interview?

column87: Q23: What have you exaggerated or misrepresented during a job interview?_My responsibilities in a previous role

column93: Q23: What have you exaggerated or misrepresented during a job interview?_My long-term career goals
